participants.reasoning: The extracted description matches the official criteria for age and newly acquired Pseudomonas aeruginosa status, with the only difference being that it refers to the analyzed cohort rather than all randomized participants.
participants.extracted: Children with cystic fibrosis aged 1–12 years with newly acquired Pseudomonas aeruginosa within 6 months of enrollment in the EPIC trial were included; for children aged 12–15 months, at least one Pa-positive culture since birth was required. A total of 282 of 304 randomized participants (93%) were analyzed in this study cohort. The cohort had a mean age of 5.8 years, and participants were predominantly pediatric patients with CF and relatively preserved lung function. Newly acquired Pa was defined as either the first lifetime documented Pa-positive culture or a Pa-positive culture after at least 2 years without Pa.
participants.official:
304 participants with CF, previously free ofP aeruginosaor had not had positive isolates for 2 years or more.
Age: 1 to 12 years.
Sex: 150 males, 154 females.

interventions.reasoning: The extracted text describes an initial open-label eradication phase plus a maintenance phase with repeated quarterly inhaled tobramycin, whereas the official text specifies 28 days of TNS with or without ciprofloxacin followed by cycled versus culture-based therapy and notes possible interim antimicrobial treatment before trial therapy.
interventions.extracted: Children with cystic fibrosis and newly acquired Pseudomonas aeruginosa received an anti-bacterial regimen aimed at eradication and maintenance. During the first study quarter, all participants were treated with 28–56 days of open-label tobramycin inhalation solution; half were additionally randomized to a 14-day course of oral ciprofloxacin and half to oral placebo. During the 15-month maintenance phase, participants were randomized to one of two strategies: cycled therapy, in which treatment was given every quarter regardless of culture results, or culture-based therapy, in which treatment was given only when quarterly respiratory cultures were positive for P. aeruginosa. Maintenance treatment in both arms consisted of 28 days of inhaled tobramycin each quarter, with the same concurrent 14-day oral ciprofloxacin or placebo assignment used initially.
interventions.official:
All participants received eradication therapy with TNS (Novartis Pharmaceutical Corp) for 28 days with or without ciprofloxacin (Bayer Healthcare AG). The main randomised intervention of TNS, with or without oral ciprofloxacin, commenced after this initial 28 days of treatment.
Intervention group: cycled therapy.
Comparator group: culture‐based therapy.

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes add a 15-month maintenance-phase framework, initial eradication at week 10, and specific recurrence measures that do not match the official primary and secondary outcomes focused on IV-therapy exacerbations, culture positivity, and additional clinical/microbiological endpoints.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure pulmonary exacerbation, assessed as time to first pulmonary exacerbation during the 15-month maintenance phase after the initial eradication quarter. Exacerbation was evaluated in two ways: pulmonary exacerbation treated with intravenous antibiotics, and pulmonary exacerbation treated with antibiotics by any route of administration (oral, inhaled, or intravenous). The study also planned microbiologic outcomes related to Pseudomonas aeruginosa, including initial eradication status at week 10 after the first-quarter antibiotic course and P. aeruginosa recurrence during the maintenance phase, measured by scheduled respiratory culture results from week 10 through the end of the 15-month maintenance phase. Baseline and follow-up clinical measures collected in the cohort included lung function (FEV1 percent predicted), body mass index, and other characteristics, but the primary outcome of this analysis was pulmonary exacerbation over 15 months, with microbiologic outcomes serving as key predictors and intermediate measures.
outcomes.official:
Primary outcomes
Time to first exacerbation requiring IV therapy.
Proportion of positive cultures in each group.
Secondary outcomes
Clinical
Time to pulmonary exacerbation not requiring IV antibiotic usage or hospitalisation
Frequency of pulmonary exacerbations, hospitalizations, and use of concomitant oral, inhaled, and IV antibiotics
Anthropometric measures (linear growth, weight gain)
